title: ranunculus orientalis subsp. tenuifolius
intro: Ranunculus orientalis subsp. tenuifolius, commonly known as the narrow-leaved buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. It is primarily used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and aiding in the treatment of skin conditions and respiratory issues. Ranunculus orientalis subsp. tenuifolius is typically prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for consumption, with caution advised due to its potential toxicity.
